creator: gahr@FreeBSD.org
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA512

Andrew Reilly wrote:
| Hi Pietro,
|
| On Mon, 30 Jun 2008 00:09:17 +0200
| Pietro Cerutti <gahr@FreeBSD.org> wrote:
|> The port is generally find. An updated version of the shar file
|> including a few modification is here:
|>
|> http://people.freebsd.org/~gahr/plt-scheme.shar
|
| Thanks, I'll check that today.  I'm working on a follow-up for
| version 4.0.1, but the plist is giving me grief...
|
|> Before committing it, I would like to ask you if you're interesting in
|> being the maintainer of the port. Generally, we would prefer not to have
|> new ports maintained by ports@.
|
| I can understand that.  What does being a "maintainer" involve?

Basically, you're responsible to keep the port up-to-date and to respond
to PRs.

| I'm not opposed, in principle, but I can't guarantee any
| particular level of extra-curricular activity above what I need
| to do anyway to make this useful for myself.  In particular, all
| of the FreeBSD systems that I run are AMD64, so my test
| environment might be atypical.

I would take the port myself, but I can barely pronounce "scheme", let
alone using it... What I can offer - and I would be happy to do so - is
test infrastructure (i386 and sparc64) and general support, if you would
agree on taking the port on your shoulder.

Please don't take me as too pushy - up to you to decide :)

Anyway, I'll wait for the follow-up including update to 4.0.1

|
| Cheers,
|
| Andrew


- --
Pietro Cerutti
gahr@FreeBSD.org
